title: Quality Gate Orchestration, Failure Semantics, and Required Checks · Pipeline Repair Bay
summary: A release responder inherits a plausible but defective quality gate dependency graph; preserve run evidence, isolate the first causal fault in quality gate orchestration, failure semantics, and required checks, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in quality gate dependency graph and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Define each formatter, lint, type, test, build, security, and policy command with deterministic inputs, exit semantics, output, and owner.
Order cheap deterministic gates before costly work while parallelizing independent evidence and preserving causal diagnosis.
Distinguish success, failure, cancelled, skipped, neutral, timed out, and continued failures in job and required-check policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership
steps.instruction:
Order the Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership delivery-system evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership, keep Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A debug pipeline hides formatter, lint, type, test, and build failures behind continue-on-error and skipped jobs while branch rules require a renamed check. Rebuild the dependency graph and evidence ownership. Competency probe: Publish concise annotations and summaries with exact command, revision, environment, failure owner, retained artifact, and rerun limits.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-gate-evidence-summary-ownership-14aage". Tailor its quality-pipeline.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership, keep Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-gate-evidence-summary-ownership-14aage
  - competency: ci-gate-evidence-summary-ownership
    artifact: quality-pipeline.yaml
    decision: gate evidence summary ownership
    environment: public fork pull request with no repository write access
    fault-injection: restore a cache written from a lower-trust branch
    retained-proof: event ref SHA workflow ref workflow SHA run ID and attempt
    case-key: 14aage
    change: |
      gates: [format-check, lint, type-check, unit, integration, build, policy]
      order: cheap-before-costly
      parallel: independent-only
      continue-on-error: false
      skipped-policy: explicit-failure
      required-check: stable-quality-name
      summary: command-revision-owner
      rerun: classified-only
    verify: inspect job results required checks skipped paths exact commands and retained failure summaries
    failure: Reject green continued failures, silent skips, mutable required names, log dumps, or unconditional reruns.
    repair: Make exit semantics explicit, preserve dependency order, fail unintended skips, stabilize checks, and assign diagnostic ownership.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
